conditions.text: A 3-phase, 440V, 50Hz, 4-pole slip ring induction motor is fed from the rotor side through an auto-transformer, and the stator is connected to a variable resistance as shown in the figure.
The motor is coupled to a 220V, separately excited D.C.
generator feeding power to a fixed resistance of 10 \( \Omega \).
The variable resistance is adjusted so that the motor runs at 1410 rpm, and the following readings were recorded: \( W_1 = 1800 \, W \), \( W_2 = -200 \, W \).

targets.text:
A 3-phase, 440 V, 50 Hz, 4-pole slip ring induction motor is fed from the rotor side through an auto-transformer, and the stator is connected to a variable resistance as shown in the figure.

The motor is coupled to a 220 V, separately excited D.C. generator feeding power to a fixed resistance of 10 \( \Omega \). Two-wattmeter method is used to measure the input power to the induction motor. The variable resistance is adjusted so that the motor runs at 1410 rpm, and the following readings were recorded: \( W_1 = 1800 \, W \), \( W_2 = -200 \, W \).

Neglecting all losses of both machines, the DC generator power output and the current through resistance \( R_{exc} \) will respectively be:

answer_slots.target_text: Neglecting all losses of both machines, what is the DC generator power output?
Neglecting all losses of both machines, what is the current through resistance \( R_{exc} \)?

sentence_segments.text: A 3-phase, 440V, 50Hz, 4-pole slip ring induction motor is fed from the rotor side through an auto-transformer, and the stator is connected to a variable resistance as shown in the figure.
The motor is coupled to a 220V, separately excited D.C.
generator feeding power to a fixed resistance of 10 \( \Omega \).
Two-wattmeter method is used to measure the input power to the induction motor.
The variable resistance is adjusted so that the motor runs at 1410 rpm, and the following readings were recorded: \( W_1 = 1800 \, W \), \( W_2 = -200 \, W \).
Neglecting all losses of both machines, the DC generator power output and the current through resistance \( R_{exc} \) will respectively be:
